506:     """
507:     A Device represents a piece of physical hardware mounted within a Rack. Each Device is assigned a DeviceType,
508:     DeviceRole, and (optionally) a Platform. Device names are not required, however if one is set it must be unique.
509: 
510:     Each Device must be assigned to a site, and optionally to a rack within that site. Associating a device with a
511:     particular rack face or unit is optional (for example, vertically mounted PDUs do not consume rack units).
512: 
513:     When a new Device is created, console/power/interface/device bay components are created along with it as dictated
514:     by the component templates assigned to its DeviceType. Components can also be added, modified, or deleted after the
515:     creation of a Device.
516:     """

967:     def _check_duplicate_component_names(self, components):
968:         """
969:         Check for duplicate component names after resolving {vc_position} placeholders.
970:         Raises AbortRequest if duplicates are found.
971:         """
972:         names = [c.name for c in components]
973:         duplicates = {n for n in names if names.count(n) > 1}
974:         if duplicates:
975:             raise AbortRequest(
976:                 _("Component name conflict after resolving {{vc_position}}: {names}").format(
977:                     names=', '.join(duplicates)
978:                 )
979:             )

981:     def _instantiate_components(self, queryset, bulk_create=True):
982:         """
983:         Instantiate components for the device from the specified component templates.
984: 
985:         Args:
986:             bulk_create: If True, bulk_create() will be called to create all components in a single query
987:                          (default). Otherwise, save() will be called on each instance individually.
988:         """
989:         model = queryset.model.component_model
990: 
991:         if bulk_create:
992:             components = [obj.instantiate(device=self) for obj in queryset]
993:             if not components:
994:                 return
995: 
996:             # Check for duplicate names after resolution {vc_position}
997:             self._check_duplicate_component_names(components)
998: 
999:             # Set default values for any applicable custom fields
1000:             if cf_defaults := CustomField.objects.get_defaults_for_model(model):
1001:                 for component in components:
1002:                     component.custom_field_data = cf_defaults
1003:             # Set denormalized references
1004:             for component in components:
1005:                 component._site = self.site
1006:                 component._location = self.location
1007:                 component._rack = self.rack
1008:             components = model.objects.bulk_create(components)
1009:             # Prefetch related objects to minimize queries needed during post_save
1010:             prefetch_fields = get_prefetchable_fields(model)
1011:             prefetch_related_objects(components, *prefetch_fields)
1012:             # Manually send the post_save signal for each of the newly created components
1013:             for component in components:
1014:                 post_save.send(
1015:                     sender=model,
1016:                     instance=component,
1017:                     created=True,
1018:                     raw=False,
1019:                     using='default',
1020:                     update_fields=None
1021:                 )
1022:         else:
1023:             components = [obj.instantiate(device=self) for obj in queryset]
1024:             if not components:
1025:                 return
1026: 
1027:             # Check for duplicate names after resolution {vc_position}
1028:             self._check_duplicate_component_names(components)
1029: 
1030:             for component in components:
1031:                 # Set default values for any applicable custom fields
1032:                 if cf_defaults := CustomField.objects.get_defaults_for_model(model):
1033:                     component.custom_field_data = cf_defaults
1034:                 component.save()
1035: 
1036:     def save(self, *args, **kwargs):
1037:         is_new = not bool(self.pk)
1038: 
1039:         # Inherit airflow attribute from DeviceType if not set
1040:         if is_new and not self.airflow:
1041:             self.airflow = self.device_type.airflow
1042: 
1043:         # Inherit default_platform from DeviceType if not set
1044:         if is_new and not self.platform:
1045:             self.platform = self.device_type.default_platform
1046: 
1047:         # Inherit location from Rack if not set
1048:         if self.rack and self.rack.location:
1049:             self.location = self.rack.location
1050: 
1051:         super().save(*args, **kwargs)
1052: 
1053:         # If this is a new Device, instantiate all the related components per the DeviceType definition
1054:         if is_new:
1055:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.consoleporttemplates.all())
1056:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.consoleserverporttemplates.all())
1057:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.powerporttemplates.all())
1058:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.poweroutlettemplates.all())
1059:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.interfacetemplates.all())
1060:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.rearporttemplates.all())
1061:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.frontporttemplates.all())
1062:             # Replicate any front/rear port mappings from the DeviceType
1063:             create_port_mappings(self, self.device_type)
1064:             # Disable bulk_create to accommodate MPTT
1065:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.modulebaytemplates.all(), bulk_create=False)
1066:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.devicebaytemplates.all())
1067:             # Disable bulk_create to accommodate MPTT
1068:             self._instantiate_components(self.device_type.inventoryitemtemplates.all(), bulk_create=False)
1069:             # Interface bridges have to be set after interface instantiation
1070:             update_interface_bridges(self, self.device_type.interfacetemplates.all())
1071: 
1072:         # Update Site and Rack assignment for any child Devices
1073:         devices = Device.objects.filter(parent_bay__device=self)
1074:         for device in devices:
1075:             device.site = self.site
1076:             device.rack = self.rack
1077:             device.location = self.location
1078:             device.save()

1223:     def delete(self, *args, **kwargs):
1224:         # Check for LAG interfaces split across member chassis
1225:         interfaces = Interface.objects.filter(
1226:             device__in=self.members.all(),
1227:             lag__isnull=False
1228:         ).exclude(
1229:             lag__device=F('device')
1230:         )
1231:         if interfaces:
1232:             raise ProtectedError(_(
1233:                 "Unable to delete virtual chassis {self}. There are member interfaces which form a cross-chassis LAG "
1234:                 "interfaces."
1235:             ).format(self=self, interfaces=InterfaceSpeedChoices))
1236: 
1237:         # Clear vc_position and vc_priority on member devices BEFORE calling super().delete()
1238:         # This must be done here because on_delete=SET_NULL executes before pre_delete signal
1239:         for device in self.members.all():
1240:             device.vc_position = None
1241:             device.vc_priority = None
1242:             device.save()
1243: 
1244:         return super().delete(*args, **kwargs)

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re
from collections import defaultdict
from pathlib import Path

scratch = Path('/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense/bench/verticals/python-django/results/kimi-for-coding_k2p7/baseline/netbox/run-scratch/opencode')
scratch.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

files = {
    'device_refs': '/Users/luc/.local/share/opencode/tool-output/tool_f3ddf07e9001ZVz37xrK5Oluvi',
    'generic_refs': '/Users/luc/.local/share/opencode/tool-output/tool_f3ddf0925001I7bAof7BQA1Bam',
    'denorm_refs': '/Users/luc/.local/share/opencode/tool-output/tool_f3ddf0a87002V2jM1AyScgFqi6',
}

entries = []
for name, path in files.items():
    with open(path) as f:
        for line in f:
            line = line.rstrip('\n')
            if not line or line.startswith('Full output saved to:'):
                continue
            m = re.match(r'^(.+?):(\d+):(.*)$', line)
            if m:
                entries.append((m.group(1), int(m.group(2)), m.group(3).strip()))

# Deduplicate by file:line
seen = set()
unique_entries = []
for path, line_no, content in entries:
    key = (path, line_no)
    if key in seen:
        continue
    seen.add(key)
    unique_entries.append((path, line_no, content))

# Group by app/subsystem
groups = defaultdict(list)
for path, line_no, content in unique_entries:
    parts = Path(path).parts
    try:
        idx = parts.index('netbox')
        app = parts[idx+1] if idx+1 < len(parts) else 'root'
    except ValueError:
        app = 'root'
    groups[app].append((path, line_no, content))

# Sort each group by path, line
for app in groups:
    groups[app].sort(key=lambda x: (x[0], x[1]))

report = []
report.append('# NetBox Device Model Contract Audit - Raw Dependents\n')
report.append(f'Total unique dependents: {len(unique_entries)}\n')

report.append('## Flat list (Step 3)\n')
for path, line_no, content in unique_entries:
    report.append(f'{path}:{line_no}')
report.append('\n')

report.append('## Grouped by subsystem (Step 4/7)\n')
for app in sorted(groups.keys()):
    report.append(f'\n### {app}\n')
    for path, line_no, content in groups[app]:
        report.append(f'{path}:{line_no}')

out_path = scratch / 'device_audit_raw.md'
out_path.write_text('\n'.join(report))
print(f'Wrote {len(unique_entries)} entries to {out_path}')

103: ):
104:     """
105:     A virtual machine which runs on a Cluster or a standalone Device.
106: 
107:     Each VM must be placed in at least one of three ways:
108: 
109:     1. Assigned to a Site alone (e.g. for logical grouping without a specific host).
110:     2. Assigned to a Cluster and optionally pinned to a host Device within that cluster.
111:     3. Assigned directly to a standalone Device (one that does not belong to any cluster).
112: 
113:     When a Cluster or Device is set, the Site is automatically inherited if not explicitly provided.
114:     If a Device belongs to a Cluster, the Cluster must also be specified on the VM.
115:     """

54: #
55: # Location/rack/device assignment
56: #
57: 
58: @receiver(post_save, sender=Location)
59: def handle_location_site_change(instance, created, **kwargs):
60:     """
61:     Update child objects if Site assignment has changed. We intentionally recurse through each child
62:     object instead of calling update() on the QuerySet to ensure the proper change records get created for each.
63:     """
64:     if not created:
65:         instance.get_descendants().update(site=instance.site)
66:         locations = instance.get_descendants(include_self=True).values_list('pk', flat=True)
67:         Rack.objects.filter(location__in=locations).update(site=instance.site)
68:         Device.objects.filter(location__in=locations).update(site=instance.site)
69:         PowerPanel.objects.filter(location__in=locations).update(site=instance.site)
70:         CableTermination.objects.filter(_location__in=locations).update(_site=instance.site)
71:         # Update component models for devices in these locations
72:         for model in COMPONENT_MODELS:
73:             model.objects.filter(device__location__in=locations).update(_site=instance.site)
74: 
75: 
76: @receiver(post_save, sender=Rack)
77: def handle_rack_site_change(instance, created, **kwargs):
78:     """
79:     Update child Devices if Site or Location assignment has changed.
80:     """
81:     if not created:
82:         Device.objects.filter(rack=instance).update(site=instance.site, location=instance.location)
83:         # Update component models for devices in this rack
84:         for model in COMPONENT_MODELS:
85:             model.objects.filter(device__rack=instance).update(
86:                 _site=instance.site,
87:                 _location=instance.location,
88:             )
89: 
90: 
91: @receiver(post_save, sender=Device)
92: def handle_device_site_change(instance, created, **kwargs):
93:     """
94:     Update child components to update the parent Site, Location, and Rack when a Device is saved.
95:     """
96:     if not created:
97:         for model in COMPONENT_MODELS:
98:             model.objects.filter(device=instance).update(
99:                 _site=instance.site,
100:                 _location=instance.location,
101:                 _rack=instance.rack,
102:             )

109: @receiver(post_save, sender=VirtualChassis)
110: def assign_virtualchassis_master(instance, created, **kwargs):
111:     """
112:     When a VirtualChassis is created, automatically assign its master device (if any) to the VC.
113:     """
114:     if created and instance.master:
115:         master = Device.objects.get(pk=instance.master.pk)
116:         master.virtual_chassis = instance
117:         master.vc_position = 1
118:         master.save()

121: @receiver(post_save, sender=VirtualChassis)
122: def update_virtualchassis_member_search_cache(instance, created, raw=False, update_fields=None, **kwargs):
123:     """
124:     Refresh the search cache for member Devices when a VirtualChassis is renamed. DeviceIndex caches
125:     virtual_chassis as its string value, so a rename would otherwise leave stale CachedValue entries.
126:     """
127:     if raw or created:
128:         return
129:     # The VC name is the only VC attribute cached on member Devices; skip saves that can't change it.
130:     if update_fields is not None and 'name' not in update_fields:
131:         return
132:     search_backend.cache(
133:         Device.objects.filter(virtual_chassis=instance).select_related('virtual_chassis'),
134:         indexer=DeviceIndex,
135:         remove_existing=True
136:     )

143: @receiver(trace_paths, sender=Cable)
144: def update_connected_endpoints(instance, created, raw=False, **kwargs):
145:     """
146:     When a Cable is saved with new terminations, retrace any affected cable paths.
147:     """
148:     logger = logging.getLogger('netbox.dcim.cable')
149:     if raw:
150:         logger.debug(f"Skipping endpoint updates for imported cable {instance}")
151:         return
152: 
153:     # Update cable paths if new terminations have been set
154:     if instance._terminations_modified:
155:         a_terminations = []
156:         b_terminations = []
157:         # Note: instance.terminations.all() is not safe to use here as it might be stale
158:         for t in CableTermination.objects.filter(cable=instance):
159:             if t.cable_end == CableEndChoices.SIDE_A:
160:                 a_terminations.append(t.termination)
161:             else:
162:                 b_terminations.append(t.termination)
163:         for nodes in [a_terminations, b_terminations]:
164:             # Examine type of first termination to determine object type (all must be the same)
165:             if not nodes:
166:                 continue
167:             if isinstance(nodes[0], PathEndpoint):
168:                 create_cablepaths(nodes)
169:             else:
170:                 rebuild_paths(nodes)
171: 
172:     # Update status of CablePaths if Cable status has been changed
173:     elif instance.status != instance._orig_status:
174:         if instance.status != LinkStatusChoices.STATUS_CONNECTED:
175:             CablePath.objects.filter(_nodes__contains=instance).update(is_active=False)
176:         else:
177:             rebuild_paths([instance])
178: 
179: 
180: @receiver(post_delete, sender=Cable)
181: def retrace_cable_paths(instance, **kwargs):
182:     """
183:     When a Cable is deleted, check for and update its connected endpoints
184:     """
185:     for cablepath in CablePath.objects.filter(_nodes__contains=instance):
186:         cablepath.retrace()
187: 
188: 
189: @receiver((post_delete, post_save), sender=PortMapping)
190: def update_passthrough_port_paths(instance, **kwargs):
191:     """
192:     When a PortMapping is created or deleted, retrace any CablePaths which traverse its front and/or rear ports.
193:     """
194:     for cablepath in CablePath.objects.filter(
195:         Q(_nodes__contains=instance.front_port) | Q(_nodes__contains=instance.rear_port)
196:     ):
197:         cablepath.retrace()
198: 
199: 
200: @receiver(post_delete, sender=CableTermination)
201: def nullify_connected_endpoints(instance, **kwargs):
202:     """
203:     Disassociate the Cable from the termination object, and retrace any affected CablePaths.
204:     """
205:     model = instance.termination_type.model_class()
206:     model.objects.filter(pk=instance.termination_id).update(cable=None, cable_end='')
207: 
208:     # If the parent Cable is being deleted in this same operation, skip the
209:     # per-termination retrace; retrace_cable_paths() will retrace each affected
210:     # path once after the Cable is deleted.
211:     if Cable._is_being_deleted(instance.cable_id):
212:         return
213: 
214:     for cablepath in CablePath.objects.filter(_nodes__contains=instance.cable):
215:         # Remove the deleted CableTermination if it's one of the path's originating nodes
216:         if instance.termination in cablepath.origins:
217:             cablepath.origins.remove(instance.termination)
218:             # Clear _path on the removed origin to prevent stale connection display
219:             model.objects.filter(pk=instance.termination_id, _path=cablepath.pk).update(_path=None)
220:         cablepath.retrace()
221:

223: @receiver(post_save, sender=Interface)
224: @receiver(post_save, sender=VMInterface)
225: def update_mac_address_interface(instance, created, raw, **kwargs):
226:     """
227:     When creating a new Interface or VMInterface, check whether a MACAddress has been designated as its primary. If so,
228:     assign the MACAddress to the interface.
229:     """
230:     if created and not raw and instance.primary_mac_address:
231:         instance.primary_mac_address.assigned_object = instance
232:         instance.primary_mac_address.save()

235: @receiver(post_save, sender=Location)
236: @receiver(post_save, sender=Site)
237: def sync_cached_scope_fields(instance, created, **kwargs):
238:     """
239:     Rebuild cached scope fields for all CachedScopeMixin-based models
240:     affected by a change in a Region, SiteGroup, Site, or Location.
241: 
242:     This method is safe to run for objects created in the past and does
243:     not rely on incremental updates. Cached fields are recomputed from
244:     authoritative relationships.
245:     """
246:     if created:
247:         return
248: 
249:     if isinstance(instance, Location):
250:         filters = {'_location': instance}
251:     elif isinstance(instance, Site):
252:         filters = {'_site': instance}
253:     else:
254:         return
255: 
256:     # These models are explicitly listed because they all subclass CachedScopeMixin
257:     # and therefore require their cached scope fields to be recomputed.
258:     for model in (Prefix, Cluster, WirelessLAN):
259:         qs = model.objects.filter(**filters)
260: 
261:         # Bulk update cached fields to avoid O(N) performance issues with large datasets.
262:         # This does not trigger post_save signals, avoiding spurious change log entries.
263:         objects_to_update = []
264:         for obj in qs:
265:             # Recompute cache using the same logic as save()
266:             obj.cache_related_objects()
267:             objects_to_update.append(obj)
268: 
269:         if objects_to_update:

1: from django.db.models.signals import post_delete, post_save, pre_delete
2: from django.dispatch import receiver
3: 
4: from dcim.models import Device
5: from virtualization.models import VirtualMachine
6: 
7: from .models import IPAddress, Prefix
8: 
9: 
10: def update_parents_children(prefix):
11:     """
12:     Update depth on prefix & containing prefixes
13:     """
14:     parents = prefix.get_parents(include_self=True).annotate_hierarchy()
15:     for parent in parents:
16:         parent._children = parent.hierarchy_children
17:     Prefix.objects.bulk_update(parents, ['_children'], batch_size=100)
18: 
19: 
20: def update_children_depth(prefix):
21:     """
22:     Update children count on prefix & contained prefixes
23:     """
24:     children = prefix.get_children(include_self=True).annotate_hierarchy()
25:     for child in children:
26:         child._depth = child.hierarchy_depth
27:     Prefix.objects.bulk_update(children, ['_depth'], batch_size=100)
28: 
29: 
30: @receiver(post_save, sender=Prefix)
31: def handle_prefix_saved(instance, created, **kwargs):
32: 
33:     # Prefix has changed (or new instance has been created)
34:     if created or instance.vrf_id != instance._vrf_id or instance.prefix != instance._prefix:
35: 
36:         update_parents_children(instance)
37:         update_children_depth(instance)
38: 
39:         # If this is not a new prefix, clean up parent/children of previous prefix
40:         if not created:
41:             old_prefix = Prefix(vrf_id=instance._vrf_id, prefix=instance._prefix)
42:             update_parents_children(old_prefix)
43:             update_children_depth(old_prefix)
44: 
45: 
46: @receiver(post_delete, sender=Prefix)
47: def handle_prefix_deleted(instance, **kwargs):
48: 
49:     update_parents_children(instance)
50:     update_children_depth(instance)
51: 
52: 
53: @receiver(pre_delete, sender=IPAddress)
54: def clear_primary_ip(instance, **kwargs):
55:     """
56:     When an IPAddress is deleted, trigger save() on any Devices/VirtualMachines for which it was a primary IP.
57:     """
58:     field_name = f'primary_ip{instance.family}'
59:     if device := Device.objects.filter(**{field_name: instance}).first():
60:         device.snapshot()
61:         setattr(device, field_name, None)
62:         device.save()
63:     if virtualmachine := VirtualMachine.objects.filter(**{field_name: instance}).first():
64:         virtualmachine.snapshot()
65:         setattr(virtualmachine, field_name, None)
66:         virtualmachine.save()
67: 
68: 
69: @receiver(pre_delete, sender=IPAddress)
70: def clear_oob_ip(instance, **kwargs):
71:     """
72:     When an IPAddress is deleted, trigger save() on any Devices for which it was a OOB IP.
73:     """
74:     if device := Device.objects.filter(oob_ip=instance).first():
75:         device.snapshot()
76:         device.oob_ip = None
77:         device.save()

1: import logging
2: 
3: from django.db.models.signals import post_delete, post_save
4: from django.dispatch import receiver
5: 
6: from dcim.exceptions import UnsupportedCablePath
7: from dcim.models import CablePath, Interface
8: from dcim.utils import create_cablepaths
9: from utilities.exceptions import AbortRequest
10: 
11: from .models import WirelessLink
12: 
13: #
14: # Wireless links
15: #
16: 
17: 
18: @receiver(post_save, sender=WirelessLink)
19: def update_connected_interfaces(instance, created, raw=False, **kwargs):
20:     """
21:     When a WirelessLink is saved, save a reference to it on each connected interface.
22:     """
23:     logger = logging.getLogger('netbox.wireless.wirelesslink')
24:     if raw:
25:         logger.debug(f"Skipping endpoint updates for imported wireless link {instance}")
26:         return
27: 
28:     if instance.interface_a.wireless_link != instance:
29:         logger.debug(f"Updating interface A for wireless link {instance}")
30:         instance.interface_a.wireless_link = instance
31:         instance.interface_a.save()
32:     if instance.interface_b.wireless_link != instance:
33:         logger.debug(f"Updating interface B for wireless link {instance}")
34:         instance.interface_b.wireless_link = instance
35:         instance.interface_b.save()
36: 
37:     # Create/update cable paths
38:     if created:
39:         for interface in (instance.interface_a, instance.interface_b):
40:             try:
41:                 create_cablepaths([interface])
42:             except UnsupportedCablePath as e:
43:                 raise AbortRequest(e)
44: 
45: 
46: @receiver(post_delete, sender=WirelessLink)
47: def nullify_connected_interfaces(instance, **kwargs):
48:     """
49:     When a WirelessLink is deleted, update its two connected Interfaces
50:     """
51:     logger = logging.getLogger('netbox.wireless.wirelesslink')
52: 
53:     if instance.interface_a is not None:
54:         logger.debug(f"Nullifying interface A for wireless link {instance}")
55:         Interface.objects.filter(pk=instance.interface_a.pk).update(wireless_link=None)
56:     if instance.interface_b is not None:
57:         logger.debug(f"Nullifying interface B for wireless link {instance}")
58:         Interface.objects.filter(pk=instance.interface_b.pk).update(wireless_link=None)
59: 
60:     # Delete and retrace any dependent cable paths
61:     for cablepath in CablePath.objects.filter(_nodes__contains=instance):
62:         cablepath.delete()

## Step 1: Orient in the codebase

NetBox is a Django-based network source-of-truth. Code is organized into apps under `netbox/`, each owning its models, API, forms, tables, views, and tests. The core device model lives in the `dcim` app.

Key locations:
- `netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:498` — `Device` class
- `netbox/dcim/` — device model, components, cables, racks, sites, modules, virtual chassis, device types/roles
- `netbox/ipam/` — IP addresses, interfaces, services, VLANs (many anchor on Device)
- `netbox/virtualization/` — clusters and VMs can pin to host Devices
- `netbox/wireless/` — wireless links cache device references
- `netbox/extras/` — config contexts, tags, custom fields, jobs, bookmarks, subscriptions, journal entries, images (generic relations that can target Device)
- `netbox/utilities/` — counter cache, query helpers, cached-count management

## Step 2: Map the device model contract

The `Device` model (`netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:498`) is a `PrimaryModel` that represents one physical piece of hardware.

Key fields and relationships:
- `device_type` (FK `dcim.DeviceType`), `role` (FK `dcim.DeviceRole`), `tenant` (FK `tenancy.Tenant`), `platform` (FK `dcim.Platform`)
- `site` (FK `dcim.Site`), `location` (FK `dcim.Location`), `rack` (FK `dcim.Rack`), `position`, `face`, `status`, `airflow`
- `primary_ip4` / `primary_ip6` / `oob_ip` (OTO `ipam.IPAddress`)
- `cluster` (FK `virtualization.Cluster`), `virtual_chassis` (FK `dcim.VirtualChassis`), `vc_position`, `vc_priority`
- `services` (GenericRelation to `ipam.Service`)
- Counter cache fields: `console_port_count`, `interface_count`, `module_bay_count`, etc.

Key methods/properties:
- `label` / `identifier` (`netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1080`)
- `primary_ip` (`netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1099`)
- `vc_interfaces(if_master=True)` (`netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1118`)
- `get_cables()` (`netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1130`)
- `get_children()` (`netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1146`)
- `total_weight` (`netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1155`)
- `save()` instantiates components from device type and propagates site/rack/location to child devices (`netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1036`)

How other code holds references to a Device:
1. Direct FK/OTO fields: `ComponentModel.device`, `Module.device`, `VirtualMachine.device`, `VirtualDeviceContext.device`, `DeviceBay.installed_device`, `VirtualChassis.master`, `CableTermination._device`, `WirelessLink._interface_a_device` / `_interface_b_device`
2. Cached/denormalized fields: `ComponentModel._site/_location/_rack`, `CableTermination._device/_rack/_location/_site`, `WirelessLink._interface_a_device/_interface_b_device`
3. Generic relations: `IPAddress.assigned_object`, `MACAddress.assigned_object`, `Service.parent_object`, `L2VPNTermination.assigned_object`, `CircuitTermination.termination`, `FHRPGroupAssignment.assigned_object`, `VLANGroup.scope`, plus `ContactAssignment`, `Bookmark`, `Subscription`, `JournalEntry`, `ImageAttachment`, `ObjectChange`, `Job`, `CustomField`, `Tag`
4. Queries and signals throughout the codebase

## Step 4: Group the dependents by subsystem

### dcim — core device model, components, cables, racks, virtual chassis
- **Device model itself**: `netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:498`
- **Direct references to Device as FK target**:
  - `VirtualChassis.master`: `netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1177`
  - `VirtualDeviceContext.device`: `netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1249`
  - `ComponentModel.device` (base for console, power, interface, etc.): `netbox/dcim/models/device_components.py:51`
  - `PortMapping.device` (front/rear port mapping): `netbox/dcim/models/device_components.py:1204`
  - `DeviceBay.installed_device`: `netbox/dcim/models/device_components.py:1468`
  - `Module.device`: `netbox/dcim/models/modules.py:254`
  - `CableTermination._device`: `netbox/dcim/models/cables.py:563`
- **Denormalized/cached device references**:
  - `ComponentModel._original_device`, `_site`, `_location`, `_rack`: `netbox/dcim/models/device_components.py:108`, `72`
  - `CableTermination._device/_rack/_location/_site`: `netbox/dcim/models/cables.py:563`
  - `Device.save()` propagates site/rack/location to child devices: `netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:1073`
- **Queries / managers**:
  - `Rack.get_rack_units`, `get_elevation`, `get_utilization`: `netbox/dcim/models/racks.py:490`, `515`, `562`, `685`
  - `DeviceType` instance count queries: `netbox/dcim/models/devices.py:329`
- **Signals**:
  - `handle_device_site_change`: `netbox/dcim/signals.py:91`
  - `handle_location_site_change` / `handle_rack_site_change` update Devices: `netbox/dcim/signals.py:68`, `82`
  - `assign_virtualchassis_master`: `netbox/dcim/signals.py:115`
  - `update_virtualchassis_member_search_cache`: `netbox/dcim/signals.py:133`
- **UI views**: `netbox/dcim/views.py:2604` (list), `2613` (detail), `2673/2674` (add/edit), `2681` (delete), `2686-2929` (component sub-views), `4497-4566` (virtual chassis)
- **Forms**: `netbox/dcim/forms/model_forms.py` (device add/edit, VC, device bay), `bulk_edit.py`, `bulk_import.py`, `filtersets.py`, `object_create.py`, `connections.py`
- **Tables**: `netbox/dcim/tables/devices.py` (DeviceTable, component tables)
- **API**: `netbox/dcim/api/views.py:419` (DeviceViewSet), `serializers_/devices.py:104`, `serializers_/device_components.py:439`
- **GraphQL**: `netbox/dcim/graphql/filters.py:206`, `graphql/types.py:248`
- **Search index**: `netbox/dcim/search.py:54`
- **SVG rendering**: `netbox/dcim/svg/racks.py`, `svg/cables.py`
- **UI panels**: `netbox/dcim/ui/panels.py`

### ipam — IP addressing, interfaces, services
- **Device FK / references**:
  - `IPAddress` assigned to interfaces (GenericFK, device via interface): `netbox/ipam/models/ip.py:949`
  - `Service` parent can be Device (GenericRelation on Device): `netbox/ipam/models/services.py:76`
- **Signals**:
  - `clear_primary_ip` / `clear_oob_ip` react to IPAddress deletion, query Device: `netbox/ipam/signals.py:59`, `74`
- **Views**: `netbox/ipam/views.py:1929` (parent device case)
- **Forms/Filtersets**: `netbox/ipam/forms/model_forms.py`, `filtersets.py`, `forms/bulk_import.py`
- **GraphQL**: `netbox/ipam/graphql/filters.py:12`, `169`

### virtualization — clusters and VMs
- **Direct FK**:
  - `VirtualMachine.device`: `netbox/virtualization/models/virtualmachines.py:140`
- **Cluster validation**:
  - `Cluster.clean()` checks host devices site/location: `netbox/virtualization/models/clusters.py:140`, `147`
- **Views**:
  - `ClusterDevicesView`: `netbox/virtualization/views.py:291`, `304`
  - `ClusterAddDevicesView`: `netbox/virtualization/views.py:369`, `372`
- **Forms/Filtersets**: `netbox/virtualization/forms/model_forms.py`, `filtersets.py`, `forms/bulk_edit.py`, `forms/bulk_import.py`, `forms/filtersets.py`

### wireless — wireless links cache device
- **Direct FK**:
  - `WirelessLink._interface_a_device` / `_interface_b_device`: `netbox/wireless/models.py:172`, `179`
  - `save()` caches parent device: `netbox/wireless/models.py:223`
- **Forms**: `netbox/wireless/forms/model_forms.py`, `forms/bulk_import.py`

## Step 5: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

Highest-risk dependents (tightly coupled, embed or cache device references):

| Rank | Dependent | Product flow | Coupling |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `dcim/models/device_components.py:51` (ComponentModel.device) | Every device component (interface, console/power port, module bay, inventory item) is attached to a parent device | Embeds FK to Device; cannot exist without it |
| 2 | `dcim/models/device_components.py:72` (ComponentModel._site/_location/_rack) | Components cache the device’s site/location/rack for fast filtering | Denormalized cache of Device fields; breaks if device-to-site contract changes |
| 3 | `dcim/models/cables.py:563` (CableTermination._device) | Cable terminations cache the device that owns the termination | Denormalized device cache; filtering and SVG rendering depend on it |
| 4 | `wireless/models.py:172` / `179` (WirelessLink._interface_a_device / _interface_b_device) | Wireless links cache the device for each interface endpoint | Denormalized device cache; breaks if how devices anchor interfaces changes |
| 5 | `virtualization/models/virtualmachines.py:140` (VirtualMachine.device) | VMs can be pinned to a host device | Direct FK; VM placement and cluster validation depend on it |
| 6 | `dcim/models/device_components.py:1468` (DeviceBay.installed_device) | Device bays can house child devices | Direct OTO; device-in-device hierarchy depends on it |
| 7 | `dcim/models/modules.py:254` (Module.device) | Modules install into device bays | Direct FK; module-to-device contract is central |
| 8 | `dcim/models/devices.py:1177` (VirtualChassis.master) | Virtual chassis master is a device | Direct OTO; multi-chassis membership depends on it |
| 9 | `ipam/signals.py:59` / `74` | IP deletion triggers device save | Reacts to device state; primary/OOB IP contract |
| 10 | `utilities/counters.py:35` | Interface count cached on Device | Counter cache field; breaks if device-interface relationship changes |

## Step 6: Surface the non-obvious dependents

These are easy to miss because they don’t look like device management code:

- `netbox/wireless/models.py:172` / `179` — Wireless link model caches the device for each interface. It lives in the wireless app, not dcim, and has no “device” in the model name.
- `netbox/dcim/models/cables.py:563` — `CableTermination` caches `_device` (and `_rack/_location/_site`) for fast filtering. It looks like a cable model, not a device model.
- `netbox/extras/jobs.py:48` — `ScriptJob` asks the router for the DB of the Device model. This is in extras/jobs, far from device logic.
- `netbox/utilities/counters.py:35` — Updates `_interface_count` on Device by issuing `Device.objects.update(...)`. Counters are infrastructure, not device UI.
- `netbox/ipam/signals.py:59` / `74` — Reacts to IPAddress deletion, not Device events, but queries and updates Device.
- `netbox/virtualization/models/clusters.py:140` / `147` — Cluster clean() validates that all host Devices match cluster site/location.
- `netbox/virtualization/views.py:369` / `372` — Cluster UI assigns/removes devices in bulk.
- `netbox/dcim/models/device_components.py:108` — `ComponentModel` caches the original device ID to prevent moving components between devices.
- `netbox/netbox/views/generic/object_views.py:512` / `bulk_views.py:1239` — Generic component-creation views treat Device as a parent model for interfaces, ports, etc.

## Step 7: Change + verification map

For each subsystem, the contract change must verify:

### dcim
- **Models**: All FK/OTO to Device (`Device`, `ComponentModel.device`, `DeviceBay.installed_device`, `Module.device`, `VirtualChassis.master`, `VirtualDeviceContext.device`, `CableTermination._device`) must continue to resolve correctly.
- **Denormalized caches**: `_site`, `_location`, `_rack`, `_device`, `_interface_count`, `_original_device` must be repopulated or invalidated when the device reference changes.
- **Signals**: `handle_device_site_change`, `handle_rack_site_change`, `assign_virtualchassis_master`, `update_virtualchassis_member_search_cache` must fire and use the correct device identity.
- **Views/Forms/API/Tables**: All `Device.objects` queries, `queryset=Device.objects.all()`, `register_model_view(Device, ...)`, and nested serializers must work.
- **Components**: `Device.save()` instantiation of components from device type and child-device propagation must still attach to the right device.

### ipam
- Verify `IPAddress` assignment and primary/OOB IP logic still finds the correct device.
- Verify `Service` generic relation to Device still works.
- Verify `clear_primary_ip` / `clear_oob_ip` signal handlers update the right device.

### virtualization
- Verify `VirtualMachine.device` FK and cluster device validation still work.
- Verify `ClusterDevicesView`, `ClusterAddDevicesView`, and cluster forms use the correct device reference.

### wireless
- Verify `WirelessLink.save()` caches the correct device for each interface endpoint.
- Verify filters and forms by device still work.

### vpn
- Verify device queryset filters and forms still resolve.

### extras
- Verify generic features (config context, tags, custom fields, contacts, bookmarks, subscriptions, journal entries, images, jobs, object changes) can still target Device.

### utilities
- Verify `calculate_cached_counts` and `counters` update the correct device fields.
- Verify any query helpers or test utilities that reference Device still work.

### netbox core generic views
- Verify generic object component views (`ObjectComponentsView`, `BulkComponentCreateView`) still treat Device as a valid parent model.
